title: Toner Cartridge Not Recognized
shortDescription: A toner cartridge is not being detected or recognized.
fullDescription:
Error 116-324 indicates the printer cannot identify an installed toner cartridge. This may be caused by an incompatible cartridge, dirty contacts on the cartridge chip, or a cartridge that is not fully seated in its slot.

When a toner cartridge is not recognized, the printer will refuse to print using that color channel. In color printers, you may still be able to print in black and white if only a color cartridge is affected.

Try removing the cartridge, cleaning the electrical contacts with a dry lint-free cloth, and reinstalling it firmly until it clicks into place.

fixSteps.title: Remove and Reseat Cartridge
fixSteps.instruction: Open the front cover, pull out the affected toner cartridge, and firmly reinstall it until it clicks into place.

fixSteps.title: Clean Chip Contacts
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the cartridge and gently wipe the electrical contacts on both the cartridge chip and the printer slot with a dry lint-free cloth.

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the printer, wait 30 seconds, and turn it back on to reset the cartridge detection.

fixSteps.title: Try a Different Cartridge
fixSteps.instruction: If the error persists, try installing a known genuine Xerox cartridge to rule out a defective or incompatible cartridge.

whenToCallPro: If multiple genuine cartridges are not recognized, the cartridge slot contacts or controller board may be faulty.

faq.q: What does Xerox error 116-324 mean?
faq.a: A toner cartridge is not being detected or recognized by the printer.

faq.q: Can I use third-party toner cartridges?
faq.a: Some third-party cartridges may trigger this error. Using genuine Xerox cartridges is recommended for compatibility.

faq.q: Do I need a service call?
faq.a: Usually not. Reseating the cartridge or cleaning contacts resolves this in most cases.

title: DHCP Address Assignment Failure
shortDescription: The printer failed to obtain an IP address via DHCP.
fullDescription:
Error 024-746 indicates the printer's network interface was unable to receive an IP address from the DHCP server. Without a valid IP address, the printer cannot communicate on the network, and all network printing, scanning, and email features will be unavailable.

This commonly occurs when the DHCP server is down, the network cable is disconnected, or the DHCP scope is exhausted with no available addresses. Wireless models may also encounter this error if the Wi-Fi signal is too weak or the wireless credentials are incorrect.

Check the network cable connection, verify the DHCP server is operational, and consider assigning a static IP address as a workaround.

causes.cause: Wi-Fi signal too weak or wrong credentials

symptoms: Network configuration page shows DHCP failure

fixSteps.title: Check Network Cable
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the Ethernet cable is securely connected to both the printer and the network switch or router. Try a different cable if available.

fixSteps.title: Restart Printer and Router
fixSteps.instruction: Power off the printer, then restart the router or network switch. Wait 2 minutes, then power the printer back on.

fixSteps.title: Verify DHCP Server
fixSteps.instruction: Confirm the DHCP server is running and has available IP addresses in its scope. Check other devices on the network to verify DHCP is functioning.

fixSteps.title: Assign Static IP
fixSteps.instruction: As a workaround, navigate to the printer's control panel network settings and manually assign a static IP address, subnet mask, and gateway.

whenToCallPro: If the network infrastructure is managed by IT and you cannot access DHCP server settings.

faq.q: What does Xerox error 024-746 mean?
faq.a: The printer failed to obtain an IP address from the DHCP server and cannot connect to the network.

faq.q: Can I use the printer without DHCP?
faq.a: Yes, you can assign a static IP address through the printer control panel as a workaround.

faq.q: Do I need a service call?
faq.a: Usually not. This is a network configuration issue, not a printer hardware problem.

title: Paper Jam in Feed Area
shortDescription: Paper is jammed in the paper feed area of the printer.
fullDescription:
Error 016-404 indicates a paper jam has occurred in the feed area of the printer. The paper may be stuck between the tray and the imaging area, preventing the print job from completing.

This error typically occurs when paper is not loaded correctly, when the paper is wrinkled or damp, or when the feed rollers are dirty or worn. Multiple sheets being fed at once can also cause this jam.

Open the front and side covers, carefully remove any jammed paper by pulling in the direction of paper travel, and check for torn fragments before closing covers and resuming.

causes.cause: Paper loaded incorrectly or fanned improperly
Paper tray overfilled beyond max line

symptoms: Grinding or crunching sounds during feed

fixSteps.title: Open Covers and Remove Paper
fixSteps.instruction: Open the front cover and any side panels. Gently pull jammed paper in the direction of paper travel. Check for torn fragments.

fixSteps.title: Inspect Paper Tray
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the paper tray, fan the paper stack, and reload it neatly. Ensure the stack does not exceed the maximum fill line. Adjust the paper guides to match the paper size.

fixSteps.title: Clean Feed Rollers
fixSteps.instruction: Dampen a lint-free cloth with water and gently wipe the rubber feed rollers to remove paper dust and debris.

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Close all covers, turn the printer off, wait 30 seconds, then turn it back on.

whenToCallPro: If paper jams recur frequently despite using correct paper and clean rollers, the feed mechanism may need professional service.

faq.q: What does Xerox error 016-404 mean?
faq.a: Paper is jammed in the feed area of the printer and must be cleared before printing can resume.

faq.q: Why do I keep getting paper jams?
faq.a: Frequent jams are usually caused by incorrect paper loading, damp paper, or worn feed rollers. Try fresh paper and clean the rollers.

faq.q: Do I need a service call?
faq.a: Not usually. Paper jams are typically user-clearable. Call a technician only if jams happen very frequently.

title: Image Quality Defect Detected
shortDescription: The printer has detected an image quality issue affecting print output.
fullDescription:
Error 016-757 indicates the printer's image quality monitoring system has detected a problem with the print output. This could manifest as streaks, bands, spots, or color shifts on printed pages.

Image quality issues can stem from depleted or damaged toner cartridges, a dirty or worn drum unit, contamination on the transfer belt, or incorrect color calibration settings. Environmental factors such as high humidity can also contribute.

Run the built-in image quality troubleshooting from the printer's control panel, which will perform automatic calibration and print test pages to diagnose the issue.
symptoms: Streaks or bands on printed pages
Spots or marks repeating on prints

fixSteps.title: Run Color Calibration
fixSteps.instruction: On the control panel, navigate to Device > Support > Image Quality and run the automatic color calibration routine.

fixSteps.title: Check Toner Levels
fixSteps.instruction: Check the toner levels on the supply status screen. Replace any cartridges that are low or empty.

fixSteps.title: Clean or Replace Drum
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the drum cartridge and inspect for scratches or contamination. Clean gently with a dry lint-free cloth or replace if worn.

fixSteps.title: Print Test Page
fixSteps.instruction: Print a test page from the control panel to verify that the image quality issue is resolved.

whenToCallPro: If image quality issues persist after replacing consumables and running calibration, the imaging system may need professional diagnosis.

faq.q: What does Xerox error 016-757 mean?
faq.a: The printer has detected an image quality problem such as streaks, banding, or color issues on printed output.

faq.q: Will running calibration fix the problem?
faq.a: Often yes. Color calibration corrects drift in color accuracy. If the issue is hardware-related, consumable replacement may also be needed.

faq.q: Do I need a service call?
faq.a: Try calibration and consumable replacement first. Call a technician if the issue persists.

title: Waste Toner Bottle Issue
shortDescription: The waste toner collection bottle is not detected or is full.
fullDescription:
Error 042-326 indicates a problem with the waste toner collection bottle. The bottle may not be installed correctly, may be full, or the sensor detecting it may be malfunctioning.

The waste toner bottle collects excess toner from the printing process. When it is full or not properly seated, the printer will halt all operations to prevent toner contamination inside the machine.

Remove the waste toner bottle, check its fill level, and reinstall it securely. Replace the bottle if it is full.

fixSteps.title: Remove Waste Toner Bottle
fixSteps.instruction: Open the front cover and carefully remove the waste toner bottle. Keep it upright to avoid spilling used toner.

fixSteps.title: Check Fill Level
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the waste toner bottle. If it is full or nearly full, replace it with a new bottle. If not full, clean the sensor area and reseat.

fixSteps.title: Clean Sensor Area
fixSteps.instruction: Wipe the waste toner bottle sensor inside the printer with a dry lint-free cloth to remove any toner dust.

fixSteps.title: Reinstall and Test
fixSteps.instruction: Insert the new or cleaned bottle firmly until it clicks into place. Close the cover and power cycle the printer.

whenToCallPro: If the error persists after replacing the waste toner bottle, the sensor or controller may need repair.

faq.q: What does Xerox error 042-326 mean?
faq.a: The waste toner collection bottle is either full, missing, or not properly installed.

faq.q: How often should I replace the waste toner bottle?
faq.a: Typically every 30,000 to 50,000 pages depending on coverage and model. The printer will alert you when replacement is needed.

faq.q: Do I need a service call?
faq.a: No, waste toner bottle replacement is a simple user-replaceable task.

title: Toner Low Warning
shortDescription: One or more toner cartridges are running low and will need replacement soon.
fullDescription:
Error 016-210 is a warning that one or more toner cartridges in the printer are nearing depletion. The printer will continue to operate normally for a period, but print quality may begin to degrade as the toner level drops further.

This is a proactive alert designed to give you time to order replacement cartridges before they run out completely. The number of remaining pages depends on your print coverage and the amount of toner remaining.

You can temporarily extend the cartridge life by removing the cartridge and gently rocking it side to side to redistribute the remaining toner evenly.

causes.cause: Normal toner consumption through regular printing
High coverage print jobs consuming toner faster

fixSteps.title: Check Toner Levels
fixSteps.instruction: On the control panel, navigate to Device > Supplies to see which cartridge is low and the estimated remaining percentage.

fixSteps.title: Redistribute Toner
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the low toner cartridge, hold it level, and gently rock it side to side 5-6 times to redistribute remaining toner. Reinstall the cartridge.

fixSteps.title: Order Replacement
fixSteps.instruction: Note the cartridge part number from the supplies page and order a genuine Xerox replacement cartridge.

fixSteps.title: Replace Cartridge When Ready
fixSteps.instruction: When the new cartridge arrives, open the front cover, remove the old cartridge, unbox the new one, remove the protective seal, and install it firmly until it clicks.

whenToCallPro: This is a consumable replacement that does not require professional service.

faq.q: What does Xerox error 016-210 mean?
faq.a: One or more toner cartridges are running low and should be replaced soon.

faq.q: Can I still print with a toner low warning?
faq.a: Yes, you can continue printing. The warning gives you time to order a replacement before the cartridge is completely empty.

faq.q: How many pages can I print after this warning?
faq.a: It varies based on your print coverage, but typically 100-500 pages remain after the initial low toner warning.

title: Toner Cartridge Empty
shortDescription: A toner cartridge is empty and must be replaced to continue printing.
fullDescription:
Error 016-211 indicates that a toner cartridge has been fully depleted. The printer will stop printing with the affected color until the cartridge is replaced. On color models, if only one color is empty, you may still be able to print in black and white.

Continuing to print with an empty cartridge can damage the drum unit, so the printer enforces this stop to protect internal components. The empty cartridge should be replaced as soon as possible.

Remove the empty cartridge, unbox the replacement, remove the protective seal tape, and install the new cartridge firmly until it clicks into the correct position.

causes.cause: Toner completely consumed through normal use
High coverage printing depleted toner prematurely

symptoms: Cartridge empty indicator on supply status

fixSteps.title: Identify Empty Cartridge
fixSteps.instruction: Check the display or navigate to Device > Supplies to identify which toner cartridge is empty.

fixSteps.title: Remove Empty Cartridge
fixSteps.instruction: Open the front cover, grasp the empty cartridge handle, and pull it straight out toward you.

fixSteps.title: Prepare New Cartridge
fixSteps.instruction: Unbox the new cartridge, remove the protective orange tape and sealing strip. Gently rock the cartridge side to side to distribute toner evenly.

fixSteps.title: Install New Cartridge
fixSteps.instruction: Slide the new cartridge into the correct slot matching the color label. Push firmly until it clicks into place. Close the front cover.

whenToCallPro: Toner replacement does not require professional service. Call a pro only if the error persists after installing a new genuine cartridge.

faq.q: What does Xerox error 016-211 mean?
faq.a: A toner cartridge is completely empty and must be replaced before printing can resume.

faq.q: Can I print in black and white if a color cartridge is empty?
faq.a: On some models yes. Check your printer settings for a black-and-white only mode when a color cartridge is depleted.

faq.q: Do I need a service call?
faq.a: No, toner replacement is a standard user task. No tools or technician required.

title: Paper Jam at Exit
shortDescription: Paper is jammed at the output or exit area of the printer.
fullDescription:
Error 016-450 indicates paper has become jammed at the exit point of the printer, where pages are delivered to the output tray. The paper may be partially visible protruding from the exit slot.

This jam typically occurs when paper curls excessively during the fusing process, when the exit rollers are dirty or worn, or when previously printed pages in the output tray are blocking the exit path.

Carefully pull the jammed paper in the direction of travel (toward the output tray) to avoid tearing. Check for any torn fragments inside the fuser exit area before resuming printing.

causes.cause: Paper curling after fusing due to humidity or paper type
Output tray full blocking exit path

symptoms: Paper jam error indicating exit area
Paper partially visible at output slot

fixSteps.title: Remove Jammed Paper from Exit
fixSteps.instruction: Gently pull the jammed paper from the exit area in the direction of paper travel. Pull steadily to avoid tearing.

fixSteps.title: Clear Output Tray
fixSteps.instruction: Remove all printed pages from the output tray to ensure the exit path is not blocked.

fixSteps.title: Check for Paper Fragments
fixSteps.instruction: Open the rear cover and inspect the fuser exit area for any torn paper fragments. Remove any pieces found.

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle and Test
fixSteps.instruction: Close all covers, power cycle the printer, and print a test page to verify the jam is cleared.

whenToCallPro: If exit jams recur frequently, the exit rollers or fuser exit guides may need professional inspection.

faq.q: What does Xerox error 016-450 mean?
faq.a: Paper is jammed at the exit or output area of the printer.

faq.q: Why does paper keep jamming at the exit?
faq.a: Common causes include humid paper that curls during fusing, a full output tray, or dirty exit rollers.

faq.q: Do I need a service call?
faq.a: Usually not. Clear the jam and check paper quality. Frequent exit jams may indicate worn rollers needing service.

title: Paper Jam in Duplex Unit
shortDescription: Paper is jammed in the automatic duplexing unit during two-sided printing.
fullDescription:
Error 016-454 indicates a paper jam has occurred within the automatic duplexing unit while performing two-sided printing. The paper gets stuck when it is being flipped over for printing on the second side.

Duplex jams are often caused by paper that is too thin, too thick, or too damp for reliable duplexing. The paper guides in the duplex path may also be dirty or the duplex rollers may be worn.

Open the rear or bottom duplex access panel, carefully remove the jammed paper, and check for any torn fragments in the duplex pathway before closing the panel and resuming.

causes.cause: Paper too thin or lightweight for duplexing
Paper curled from humidity or fusing heat

symptoms: Paper stuck in rear/bottom duplex area
Only one side of page printed

fixSteps.title: Access Duplex Unit
fixSteps.instruction: Open the rear cover or remove the duplex unit from the bottom of the printer to access the jammed paper.

fixSteps.title: Remove Jammed Paper
fixSteps.instruction: Carefully pull the jammed paper out in the direction of paper travel. Check for any torn fragments and remove them.

fixSteps.title: Check Paper Type
fixSteps.instruction: Verify you are using paper between 60-105 gsm for duplexing. Replace with appropriate weight paper if needed.

fixSteps.title: Reseat Duplex Unit
fixSteps.instruction: If the duplex unit is removable, take it out and firmly reinstall it. Close all covers and power cycle the printer.

whenToCallPro: If duplex jams occur frequently despite using correct paper weight, the duplex mechanism may need professional repair.

faq.q: What does Xerox error 016-454 mean?
faq.a: Paper is jammed inside the automatic duplexing unit during two-sided printing.

faq.q: What paper weight works for duplexing?
faq.a: Most Xerox printers support duplexing with paper between 60 and 105 gsm. Check your model's specifications.

faq.q: Do I need a service call?
faq.a: Not usually. Clear the jam and use appropriate paper. Frequent jams may indicate a worn duplex mechanism.

title: Authentication Server Error
shortDescription: The printer cannot communicate with the authentication server.
fullDescription:
Error 024-969 indicates the printer failed to connect to the configured authentication server (such as LDAP, Active Directory, or Kerberos) when a user attempted to log in or access a secure feature.

This error prevents users from accessing secure print, scan-to-email, or other authenticated functions. The authentication server may be down, the network connection between the printer and server may be broken, or the server credentials configured on the printer may be incorrect.

Verify the authentication server is running, check network connectivity between the printer and the server, and review the authentication settings in the printer's Embedded Web Server (EWS).

causes.cause: Incorrect server address or port configured
Network connectivity issue between printer and server

fixSteps.title: Verify Server Status
fixSteps.instruction: Confirm the authentication server (LDAP/Active Directory) is running and accessible from other devices on the network.

fixSteps.title: Check Network Connectivity
fixSteps.instruction: From the printer's control panel, run a network connectivity test or ping the server address to verify the printer can reach it.

fixSteps.title: Review Authentication Settings
fixSteps.instruction: Access the printer's Embedded Web Server via a browser, navigate to Properties > Connectivity > Authentication, and verify the server address, port, and bind credentials are correct.

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle Printer
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the printer, wait 30 seconds, and turn it back on. Attempt authentication again.

whenToCallPro: If you do not have access to the authentication server configuration or the printer's admin settings, contact your IT department.

faq.q: What does Xerox error 024-969 mean?
faq.a: The printer cannot connect to the authentication server needed for secure login and features.

faq.q: Can I still print without authentication?
faq.a: If your environment requires authentication, you cannot access secure features. Basic printing via USB may still work if enabled.

faq.q: Do I need a service call?
faq.a: This is typically a network/server configuration issue. Contact your IT administrator rather than a printer technician.

title: Transfer Belt Error
shortDescription: The transfer belt assembly has malfunctioned or failed.
fullDescription:
Error 010-317 indicates a failure in the transfer belt assembly, which is responsible for transferring toner images from the drum cartridges onto the paper. When the transfer belt fails, print quality is severely affected or printing stops entirely.

The transfer belt may have reached its rated lifespan, may be mechanically jammed, or its drive motor may have failed. In color printers, a failed transfer belt causes complete color misregistration or blank output.

Power cycle the printer first. If the error persists, the transfer belt unit will need to be replaced. This is a user-replaceable component on most VersaLink and Phaser models.

causes.cause: Transfer belt reached end of life
Foreign object on transfer belt surface

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the printer, wait 60 seconds, and turn it back on. Some transfer belt errors are caused by temporary sensor issues.

fixSteps.title: Inspect Transfer Belt
fixSteps.instruction: Open the front cover and visually inspect the transfer belt for damage, debris, or misalignment. Remove any visible foreign objects.

fixSteps.title: Reseat Transfer Belt
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the transfer belt unit by lifting the release levers and pulling it out. Inspect the surface and reinstall it firmly.

fixSteps.title: Replace Transfer Belt
fixSteps.instruction: If the belt is worn or damaged, install a new transfer belt unit. Follow the instructions included with the replacement part.

whenToCallPro: If the error persists after replacing the transfer belt, the drive motor or controller board may need professional diagnosis.

faq.q: What does Xerox error 010-317 mean?
faq.a: The transfer belt assembly has failed and likely needs replacement.

faq.q: How long does a transfer belt last?
faq.a: Typically 100,000 to 200,000 pages depending on the model and usage patterns.

faq.q: Can I replace the transfer belt myself?
faq.a: Yes, on most Xerox models the transfer belt is a user-replaceable component that slides in and out of the front.

title: Imaging Unit Error
shortDescription: The imaging unit (drum) has malfunctioned or is not detected.
fullDescription:
Error 077-316 indicates a problem with one of the imaging units (drum cartridges) in the printer. The imaging unit may not be detected, may be improperly installed, or may have a communication error with the printer's controller.

Imaging units are photosensitive drums that create the electrostatic image transferred to paper. When an imaging unit fails, the affected color channel produces no output or severely degraded output.

Remove and reinstall the affected imaging unit, ensuring the contacts are clean and the unit is fully seated. If the error persists, the imaging unit may need replacement.

causes.cause: Dirty electrical contacts on imaging unit
Imaging unit reached end of life

symptoms: Vertical lines or streaks on prints

fixSteps.title: Reseat Imaging Unit
fixSteps.instruction: Open the front cover, remove the affected imaging unit by its handle, and firmly reinstall it until it clicks into place.

fixSteps.title: Clean Contacts
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the imaging unit and gently wipe the electrical contacts on the unit and inside the printer slot with a dry lint-free cloth.

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the printer, wait 30 seconds, and power it back on. The printer will re-detect the imaging unit during startup.

fixSteps.title: Replace Imaging Unit
fixSteps.instruction: If the error persists, replace the imaging unit with a new genuine Xerox part. Avoid exposing the drum surface to direct light during installation.

whenToCallPro: If multiple imaging units are failing simultaneously or the error persists after replacement, the printer's imaging controller may need diagnosis.

faq.q: What does Xerox error 077-316 mean?
faq.a: An imaging unit (drum cartridge) is not working properly or is not being detected by the printer.

faq.q: Is the imaging unit the same as the toner cartridge?
faq.a: No. The imaging unit (drum) is a separate component from the toner cartridge. Both need periodic replacement but at different intervals.

faq.q: Do I need a service call?
faq.a: Try reseating and cleaning contacts first. Imaging unit replacement is a user-serviceable task on most models.

title: Fuser Exit Sensor Error
shortDescription: The fuser exit sensor has detected a malfunction or paper stoppage.
fullDescription:
Error 010-320 indicates the sensor at the fuser exit has detected an abnormality. This sensor monitors paper as it exits the fuser assembly after toner has been bonded to the page. If paper fails to exit the fuser within the expected time, or the sensor itself malfunctions, this error is triggered.

The issue may be a paper jam at the fuser exit, a dirty or failing sensor, or a mechanical problem with the fuser exit rollers. In some cases, the error is caused by label stock or thick media that moves more slowly through the fuser.

Check for any paper stuck at the fuser exit area, clean the sensor if accessible, and verify you are using supported media types.

causes.cause: Paper jammed at fuser exit point
Fuser exit sensor dirty or obstructed
Fuser exit roller worn or damaged
Thick or specialty media causing slow exit

symptoms: Prints wrinkled or stuck at exit

fixSteps.title: Clear Fuser Exit Area
fixSteps.instruction: Open the rear cover and carefully remove any paper stuck at the fuser exit. Use caution as the fuser area may be hot.

fixSteps.title: Inspect for Debris
fixSteps.instruction: Look for torn paper fragments, label adhesive residue, or other debris near the fuser exit sensor and rollers. Remove any found.

fixSteps.title: Check Media Type
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the paper or media loaded is within the printer's supported weight and type specifications. Avoid unsupported label stock.

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Close all covers, turn the printer off for 60 seconds, then turn it back on. Print a test page to verify the error is cleared.

whenToCallPro: If the error recurs after clearing jams and the fuser exit sensor or rollers appear damaged, professional service is recommended.

faq.q: What does Xerox error 010-320 mean?
faq.a: The fuser exit sensor detected a problem, usually a paper stoppage or sensor malfunction at the fuser exit.

faq.q: Is it safe to touch the fuser area?
faq.a: Use caution. The fuser operates at high temperatures. Let the printer cool for a few minutes before reaching into the fuser exit area.

faq.q: Do I need a service call?
faq.a: Clear jams first. If the error recurs without jammed paper, a sensor or roller replacement may be needed.

title: Fuser Heat Error
shortDescription: The fuser unit is not reaching or maintaining the required temperature.
fullDescription:
Error 010-318 indicates the fuser unit cannot reach its target operating temperature or is unable to maintain it during printing. The fuser must reach a specific temperature to properly bond toner to paper, and when it cannot, print quality suffers or the printer halts.

This error can be caused by a failing fuser heating element, a defective thermistor (temperature sensor), a faulty power supply, or simply an inadequate electrical supply to the printer. In cold environments, it may take longer for the fuser to warm up.

Allow the printer to warm up fully, ensure it is connected to a dedicated electrical outlet (not a power strip), and power cycle it. If the error persists, the fuser unit likely needs replacement.
symptoms: Toner smears or rubs off printed pages
Printer takes very long to warm up
Print jobs fail with fuser error

fixSteps.title: Check Power Connection
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the printer is plugged directly into a wall outlet, not through a power strip or extension cord. Verify the outlet provides stable power.

fixSteps.title: Allow Full Warm-Up
fixSteps.instruction: Turn the printer off, wait 5 minutes, then turn it back on. Allow at least 3-5 minutes for the fuser to reach operating temperature.

fixSteps.title: Reseat Fuser Unit
fixSteps.instruction: If accessible, open the rear cover, release the fuser retention levers, remove the fuser, and reinstall it firmly. Caution: the fuser may be hot.

fixSteps.title: Replace Fuser Unit
fixSteps.instruction: If the error persists, the fuser heating element has likely failed. Replace the fuser unit with a genuine Xerox replacement.

whenToCallPro: If the fuser heat error persists after reseating and the fuser element has failed, professional replacement is recommended unless you are comfortable replacing the fuser yourself.

faq.q: What does Xerox error 010-318 mean?
faq.a: The fuser unit is unable to reach or maintain the temperature needed to bond toner to paper.

faq.q: Can a power strip cause this error?
faq.a: Yes. Power strips can limit voltage to the printer. Always connect directly to a wall outlet.

faq.q: Do I need a service call?
faq.a: If reseating the fuser and checking power does not help, the fuser likely needs replacement which can be done by the user or a technician.

title: IBT Belt Life Warning
shortDescription: The intermediate transfer belt (IBT) is nearing end of life.
fullDescription:
Error 092-310 is a maintenance warning indicating the Intermediate Belt Transfer (IBT) unit is approaching or has reached its rated page life. The IBT belt transfers toner from the photoconductor drums to the paper in color laser printers.

As the IBT belt wears, you may notice gradual color quality degradation including banding, ghosting, or color registration shifts. The printer will continue to operate for a period after this warning, but the belt should be replaced soon to maintain output quality.

Order a replacement IBT belt unit and plan for installation. The belt is a user-replaceable component on most Xerox color models.

causes.cause: IBT belt reached rated page life
High print volume accelerated belt wear
Belt surface degradation from normal use

symptoms: Ghosting or shadow images on output

fixSteps.title: Check Belt Status
fixSteps.instruction: Navigate to Device > Supplies on the control panel to check the remaining life percentage of the IBT belt.

fixSteps.title: Order Replacement
fixSteps.instruction: Note the IBT belt part number for your model and order a genuine Xerox replacement.

fixSteps.title: Remove Old Belt
fixSteps.instruction: Open the front cover, remove the toner cartridges, release the IBT belt retention levers, and carefully slide out the old belt unit.

fixSteps.title: Install New Belt
fixSteps.instruction: Slide the new IBT belt unit into position, secure the retention levers, reinstall all toner cartridges, and close the front cover. The printer will automatically reset the belt counter.

whenToCallPro: IBT belt replacement is user-serviceable, but call a technician if you are uncomfortable removing internal components or if the new belt does not resolve quality issues.

faq.q: What does Xerox error 092-310 mean?
faq.a: The intermediate transfer belt (IBT) is nearing end of life and should be replaced to maintain print quality.

faq.q: Can I keep printing after this warning?
faq.a: Yes, for a limited number of pages. However, print quality will degrade. Plan to replace the belt soon.

faq.q: How long does the IBT belt last?
faq.a: Typically 100,000 to 200,000 pages depending on the model and print volume.

title: Waste Toner Container Full
shortDescription: The waste toner container has reached capacity and must be replaced.
fullDescription:
Error 042-359 indicates the waste toner collection container is completely full. The printer will stop all print operations until the container is replaced to prevent toner from spilling inside the machine and causing damage.

The waste toner container collects excess and unused toner from the printing process. It fills gradually over the life of the printer and is a normal maintenance item. Replacing the waste toner container is simple and does not require any tools.

Purchase a replacement waste toner container for your model, remove the full container carefully (keeping it upright to avoid spilling), and install the new container.

causes.cause: Waste toner container reached maximum capacity
High print volume filled container faster
High coverage prints generating more waste toner

symptoms: Waste toner full message on display
Printer refuses to start print jobs

fixSteps.title: Open Access Panel
fixSteps.instruction: Open the front or side cover to access the waste toner container. The location varies by model.

fixSteps.title: Remove Full Container
fixSteps.instruction: Carefully remove the full waste toner container, keeping it upright. Place it in the bag provided with the replacement.

fixSteps.title: Install New Container
fixSteps.instruction: Unbox the new waste toner container and slide it into position until it locks into place.

fixSteps.title: Close and Test
fixSteps.instruction: Close the access panel. The printer will automatically detect the new container and reset the counter. Print a test page.

whenToCallPro: Waste toner container replacement is a simple user task. No professional service is needed.

faq.q: What does Xerox error 042-359 mean?
faq.a: The waste toner container is completely full and must be replaced before printing can continue.

faq.q: Can I empty the waste toner container and reuse it?
faq.a: This is not recommended. The container includes a counter chip that may not reset, and handling waste toner can be messy and unhealthy.

faq.q: Do I need a service call?
faq.a: No. This is one of the simplest maintenance tasks and requires no tools.

title: Finisher Error
shortDescription: The finisher accessory has encountered a mechanical error.
fullDescription:
Error 005-310 indicates a malfunction in the attached finisher unit, which handles stapling, hole punching, collating, or booklet-making functions. The finisher may have a paper jam, a staple jam, or a mechanical failure in one of its components.

Finisher errors prevent the printer from completing jobs that require finishing operations. Standard print jobs without finishing may still work if you change the output destination to the main output tray.

Check the finisher for paper jams and staple jams. Ensure the staple cartridge is properly installed and not empty. Power cycle the printer with the finisher attached to reset the finisher controller.

causes.cause: Staple jam or empty staple cartridge
Finisher not properly connected to printer

symptoms: Finishing options grayed out in driver

fixSteps.title: Check for Paper Jams
fixSteps.instruction: Open all finisher access doors and remove any jammed paper. Check all paper paths within the finisher.

fixSteps.title: Check Staple Cartridge
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the staple cartridge, check for jammed staples, and clear any visible staple jams. Reinstall or replace the cartridge if empty.

fixSteps.title: Reseat Finisher Connection
fixSteps.instruction: If the finisher is detachable, disconnect it from the printer and reconnect it, ensuring all cables and connectors are firmly seated.

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the printer and finisher, wait 60 seconds, then turn both back on. The finisher should reinitialize.

whenToCallPro: If the finisher error persists after clearing jams and staple issues, a mechanical component inside the finisher may need professional repair.

faq.q: What does Xerox error 005-310 mean?
faq.a: The finisher accessory has a mechanical error, usually a paper or staple jam.

faq.q: Can I print without the finisher working?
faq.a: Yes, change the output destination to the main output tray and disable stapling in the print driver.

faq.q: Do I need a service call?
faq.a: Clear jams and check staples first. If the error persists, a technician may need to inspect the finisher mechanism.

title: LDAP Server Connection Error
shortDescription: The printer cannot connect to the configured LDAP directory server.
fullDescription:
Error 024-924 indicates the printer is unable to establish a connection to the LDAP (Lightweight Directory Access Protocol) server used for address book lookups, user authentication, or email address verification.

This error typically appears when using scan-to-email and the printer needs to look up email addresses, or during user login if LDAP authentication is enabled. The LDAP server may be down, the connection settings may be incorrect, or a firewall may be blocking the LDAP port.

Verify the LDAP server settings in the printer's Embedded Web Server, test the connection from another device, and check that port 389 (LDAP) or 636 (LDAPS) is not blocked.

causes.cause: Incorrect LDAP server address or port
LDAP bind credentials incorrect or expired

symptoms: LDAP error when searching address book
Cannot look up email addresses on printer

fixSteps.title: Verify LDAP Server Status
fixSteps.instruction: Confirm the LDAP/Active Directory server is running and accessible from other devices on the network.

fixSteps.title: Check LDAP Configuration
fixSteps.instruction: Access the printer's Embedded Web Server, navigate to Properties > Connectivity > Protocols > LDAP, and verify the server address, port, search base, and bind credentials.

fixSteps.title: Test LDAP Connection
fixSteps.instruction: Use the LDAP connection test feature in the EWS if available, or try an LDAP search from the printer's control panel to test the connection.

fixSteps.title: Check Firewall Rules
fixSteps.instruction: Verify that firewall rules are not blocking traffic on LDAP port 389 or LDAPS port 636 between the printer's IP and the LDAP server.

whenToCallPro: Contact your IT administrator if you do not have access to the LDAP server or printer admin settings.

faq.q: What does Xerox error 024-924 mean?
faq.a: The printer cannot connect to the LDAP directory server used for address book lookups and authentication.

faq.q: Can I still use scan-to-email without LDAP?
faq.a: Yes, you can manually type email addresses instead of searching the LDAP directory.

faq.q: Do I need a service call?
faq.a: This is a network/server configuration issue. Contact your IT department rather than a printer technician.

title: Memory Full - Print Job Cancelled
shortDescription: The printer has run out of memory and cancelled the current print job.
fullDescription:
Error 016-783 indicates the printer's available memory has been exhausted while processing a print job, forcing the job to be cancelled. This typically occurs when printing very large or complex documents, high-resolution images, or multiple large jobs simultaneously.

The printer allocates memory for processing each print job, including rasterizing pages, holding job data, and managing print queues. When a job requires more memory than is available, the printer cannot complete the rendering and must cancel the job.

Reduce the complexity of the print job by lowering the resolution, splitting the document into smaller parts, or clearing the print queue of pending jobs to free memory.

causes.cause: Print job too large or complex for available memory
Multiple large jobs in print queue consuming memory
High-resolution images in document exceeding memory

symptoms: Only partial pages printed before error

fixSteps.title: Clear Print Queue
fixSteps.instruction: On the control panel, navigate to Jobs and delete all pending print jobs to free memory.

fixSteps.title: Reduce Print Resolution
fixSteps.instruction: In the print driver settings, reduce the print resolution from 1200 dpi to 600 dpi or lower to reduce memory requirements.

fixSteps.title: Split Document
fixSteps.instruction: Divide the document into smaller sections and print each section separately. For example, print pages 1-10, then 11-20.

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the printer, wait 30 seconds, then turn it back on to clear all memory and reset the print system.

whenToCallPro: If memory errors occur frequently with normal-sized documents, the printer may need a RAM upgrade (if supported) or professional diagnosis.

faq.q: What does Xerox error 016-783 mean?
faq.a: The printer ran out of memory while processing a print job and had to cancel it.

faq.q: How can I prevent memory full errors?
faq.a: Print at lower resolution, split large documents, and avoid queuing multiple complex jobs simultaneously.

faq.q: Can I add more memory to my printer?
faq.a: Some models support memory upgrades. Check your model's specifications or contact Xerox support.

title: NVRAM Read/Write Error
shortDescription: The printer's NVRAM (non-volatile memory) has a read or write failure.
fullDescription:
Error 091-402 indicates a failure in the printer's NVRAM (Non-Volatile Random Access Memory), which stores critical configuration data, supply counters, network settings, and calibration values. When NVRAM fails, the printer may lose its settings, display incorrect supply levels, or be unable to start properly.

NVRAM errors can be caused by power surges, firmware corruption, or hardware degradation of the memory chip. In some cases, a factory reset can resolve software-level NVRAM corruption, but hardware-level failures require board replacement.

Try a power cycle first, then attempt an NVRAM initialization (factory reset) from the service menu. Back up your settings via the EWS before resetting if possible.

causes.cause: NVRAM data corruption from power surge

symptoms: Printer fails to start or boot loops

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Turn the printer off using the power button, unplug it from the wall, wait 60 seconds, plug it back in, and turn it on.

fixSteps.title: Backup Settings
fixSteps.instruction: If the printer is accessible, log into the Embedded Web Server and export the configuration backup from Properties > General Setup > Cloning.

fixSteps.title: NVRAM Initialization
fixSteps.instruction: Access the service menu (hold specific key combination during boot as documented for your model) and select NVRAM initialization to reset the memory to factory defaults.

fixSteps.title: Restore Settings
fixSteps.instruction: After NVRAM reset, reconfigure the printer settings manually or restore from the configuration backup created earlier.

whenToCallPro: If NVRAM initialization does not resolve the error, the controller board NVRAM chip may have failed and requires professional board-level repair or replacement.

faq.q: What does Xerox error 091-402 mean?
faq.a: The printer's non-volatile memory (NVRAM) has a read or write error, which may affect settings and operation.

faq.q: Will I lose my settings if I reset NVRAM?
faq.a: Yes, an NVRAM reset restores factory defaults. Back up your configuration through the EWS before resetting.

faq.q: Do I need a service call?
faq.a: Try a power cycle and NVRAM reset first. If the error persists, the controller board may need professional replacement.

title: Fuser Standby Temperature Error
shortDescription: The fuser cannot maintain its standby temperature.
fullDescription:
Error 010-370 indicates the fuser unit is unable to maintain its low-power standby temperature. In standby mode, the fuser stays at a reduced temperature so it can quickly warm up when a print job arrives. If the standby temperature cannot be maintained, the printer may not be ready to print when needed.

This error can be caused by a degrading fuser heating element, a failing standby temperature sensor, or fluctuating power supply. In some cases, energy-saving settings may conflict with the fuser's ability to maintain standby temperature.

Check the power connection, disable extreme energy-saving modes, and power cycle the printer. If the error recurs, the fuser may need replacement.
symptoms: Intermittent fuser errors during idle periods

fixSteps.title: Check Power Supply
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the printer is connected directly to a wall outlet, not through a power strip. Verify the outlet provides stable voltage.

fixSteps.title: Adjust Energy Saving
fixSteps.instruction: On the control panel, navigate to Device > General > Energy Saver and set the timeout to a longer interval or disable deep sleep mode.

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the printer, unplug it for 2 minutes, then plug it back in and turn it on. Allow a full warm-up cycle.

fixSteps.title: Replace Fuser if Needed
fixSteps.instruction: If the error persists, the fuser unit may need replacement. Order a genuine Xerox fuser and install following the model-specific instructions.

whenToCallPro: If the error persists after checking power and adjusting settings, the fuser or its temperature sensor may need professional replacement.

faq.q: What does Xerox error 010-370 mean?
faq.a: The fuser unit cannot maintain its standby temperature, which may delay printing readiness.

faq.q: Can energy saving modes cause this error?
faq.a: In some cases, very aggressive deep sleep settings can interfere with fuser standby temperature maintenance.

faq.q: Do I need a service call?
faq.a: Try power and energy settings adjustments first. Persistent errors likely indicate a fuser nearing end of life.

title: Print Overrun Error
shortDescription: The printer cannot process the page data fast enough, causing a print overrun.
fullDescription:
Error 016-799 in the context of a print overrun means the printer's processor cannot render the page data fast enough to keep up with the print engine speed. This results in missing data on the printed page, typically blank bands or missing sections at the bottom of complex pages.

Print overruns are most common with very complex documents containing high-resolution images, intricate vector graphics, transparency effects, or embedded fonts. The printer's RIP (Raster Image Processor) runs out of time before the page data is fully rendered.

Reduce the print resolution, simplify the document, or enable the printer's page buffering mode (if available) to allow more time for processing complex pages.

causes.cause: Document too complex for printer's processor
Printer firmware needs updating for optimization

symptoms: Missing content at bottom of complex pages
Print overrun or data overrun message

fixSteps.title: Reduce Print Resolution
fixSteps.instruction: In the print driver, change the resolution from 1200 dpi to 600 dpi to reduce processing requirements.

fixSteps.title: Simplify Document
fixSteps.instruction: Flatten transparency layers, reduce image resolution in the source document, and convert complex vector graphics to raster images before printing.

fixSteps.title: Print as Image
fixSteps.instruction: In the print dialog, enable 'Print as Image' option (in Adobe Reader: Advanced > Print as Image). This pre-renders the page and reduces RIP complexity.

fixSteps.title: Update Firmware
fixSteps.instruction: Check for and install the latest firmware from the Xerox support website. Firmware updates often include RIP performance improvements.

whenToCallPro: This is typically not a hardware issue. If overruns occur on simple documents, the printer may need professional diagnosis of its processor or memory.

faq.q: What is a print overrun error?
faq.a: It means the printer's processor cannot render page content fast enough, resulting in missing or blank areas on the print.

faq.q: Why do only complex pages have this problem?
faq.a: Complex documents with high-resolution images, transparency, and vector graphics require more processing power to rasterize.

faq.q: Do I need a service call?
faq.a: Usually not. Reducing resolution or simplifying the document resolves the issue without any hardware service.

title: DNS Resolution Failure
shortDescription: The printer cannot resolve hostnames through the configured DNS server.
fullDescription:
Error 024-747 can also indicate a DNS resolution failure where the printer is connected to the network with a valid IP address but cannot resolve hostnames. This affects features that rely on server names rather than IP addresses, such as scan-to-email (SMTP server by hostname), LDAP lookups, and firmware update checks.

When DNS fails, the printer can still print from the local network using IP addresses, but any feature requiring hostname resolution will fail. The DNS server may be unreachable, incorrectly configured, or not responding to the printer's queries.

Verify the DNS server settings on the printer, test DNS from other network devices, and consider adding a secondary DNS server as a fallback.

causes.cause: DNS server address incorrect or not configured
DNS server offline or not responding
DNS server rejecting queries from printer IP

symptoms: Network features work with IP but not hostname

fixSteps.title: Check DNS Configuration
fixSteps.instruction: On the control panel, navigate to Device > Connectivity > Ethernet > TCP/IP and verify the primary and secondary DNS server addresses are correct.

fixSteps.title: Test DNS from Other Devices
fixSteps.instruction: Verify that other devices on the same network can resolve hostnames using the same DNS server to rule out a server-side issue.

fixSteps.title: Add Secondary DNS
fixSteps.instruction: Configure a secondary DNS server (such as 8.8.8.8 for Google Public DNS) as a fallback in the printer's network settings.

fixSteps.title: Use IP Addresses Instead
fixSteps.instruction: As a workaround, replace any hostnames in the printer configuration (SMTP server, LDAP server) with their direct IP addresses.

whenToCallPro: If DNS issues affect the entire network, contact your IT department or network administrator.

faq.q: What is DNS resolution failure?
faq.a: DNS translates server names (like mail.company.com) into IP addresses. When it fails, the printer cannot find servers by name.

faq.q: Can I still print if DNS fails?
faq.a: Yes, basic printing over the network still works. Only features that need hostname resolution are affected.

faq.q: Do I need a service call?
faq.a: No, this is a network configuration issue. Adjust DNS settings on the printer or contact your IT team.

title: Fuser Overheat Protection
shortDescription: The fuser has triggered its overheat protection and shut down.
fullDescription:
Error 010-351 can indicate the fuser has exceeded its maximum safe operating temperature, triggering the thermal protection circuit. This is a safety mechanism that shuts down the fuser to prevent fire risk or damage to the printer.

Fuser overheating can occur when the temperature control circuit malfunctions, the thermistor provides incorrect readings, or the fuser heating element fails to cycle off properly. Environmental factors like high ambient temperature or blocked ventilation can contribute.

Immediately power off the printer, let it cool for at least 30 minutes, ensure adequate ventilation around the printer, and verify the air vents are not blocked. Do not attempt to print while the fuser is overheated.
symptoms: Very hot to touch near fuser area

fixSteps.title: Power Off Immediately
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the printer immediately using the power switch. If the smell of burning is strong, unplug the printer from the wall.

fixSteps.title: Allow Cooling Period
fixSteps.instruction: Let the printer cool for at least 30 minutes. Do not open covers while the fuser is extremely hot.

fixSteps.title: Check Ventilation
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the printer has at least 4 inches of clearance on all sides. Remove any items blocking the ventilation openings.

fixSteps.title: Power On and Test
fixSteps.instruction: After cooling, turn the printer back on and print a test page. If the error returns, the fuser temperature control has failed and needs replacement.

whenToCallPro: If the fuser overheats repeatedly, the temperature control circuit has failed. Call a technician to replace the fuser and inspect for damage.

faq.q: Is a fuser overheat dangerous?
faq.a: The thermal protection circuit prevents fire risk by shutting down the fuser. Always turn off the printer immediately and let it cool.

faq.q: Can I keep using the printer after it cools?
faq.a: If the error clears after cooling and was caused by poor ventilation, yes. If it recurs, stop using the printer until the fuser is replaced.

faq.q: Do I need a service call?
faq.a: If the overheat occurs more than once, yes. A failing temperature control circuit is a safety concern that requires professional repair.

title: Drum Cartridge Not Recognized
shortDescription: A drum (imaging) cartridge is not being detected by the printer.
fullDescription:
Error 116-324 for drum cartridges indicates the printer cannot identify an installed imaging drum unit. This may be because the drum is not fully seated, the chip contacts are dirty, or the drum cartridge is incompatible with the printer model.

Drum cartridges have electronic chips that communicate capacity and compatibility information to the printer. When this communication fails, the printer cannot verify the drum and will not print using the affected color channel.

Remove the drum cartridge, inspect and clean the chip contacts, and reinstall it firmly. Avoid touching the drum surface as oils from skin can damage the photosensitive coating.

causes.cause: Drum cartridge not fully seated in slot
Incompatible drum cartridge for this model

symptoms: Supply status shows drum as missing

fixSteps.title: Reseat Drum Cartridge
fixSteps.instruction: Open the front cover, remove the affected drum cartridge by its handle, and reinstall it firmly until you feel it click into place.

fixSteps.title: Clean Chip Contacts
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the drum and gently wipe the electronic chip contacts with a dry lint-free cloth. Also clean the matching contacts inside the printer.

fixSteps.title: Verify Compatibility
fixSteps.instruction: Check the drum cartridge part number against your printer model's specifications to ensure compatibility.

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle
fixSteps.instruction: After reseating the drum, close all covers and power cycle the printer. The drum detection runs during startup.

whenToCallPro: If multiple genuine drum cartridges are not recognized, the printer's drum slot or controller may need professional diagnosis.

faq.q: What does error 116-324 mean for drum cartridges?
faq.a: The printer cannot detect or communicate with an installed drum (imaging) cartridge.

faq.q: Can I use third-party drum cartridges?
faq.a: Third-party drums may not have compatible chips. Genuine Xerox drums are recommended for reliable detection.

faq.q: Should I touch the drum surface?
faq.a: Never touch the green or blue drum surface. Oils from your fingers can permanently damage the photosensitive coating.

title: Scanner Carriage Error
shortDescription: The scanner carriage is stuck or cannot move to its home position.
fullDescription:
Error 027-452 can also indicate the scanner carriage mechanism is jammed or cannot return to its home position. The scanner carriage holds the light source and sensor array and must move freely across the flatbed glass to scan documents.

This can be caused by a foreign object on the scanner glass blocking the carriage, a loose scanner lock (shipping lock left engaged), or a worn carriage drive belt. If the ADF is used, paper fragments from the document feeder can also obstruct the carriage.

Open the scanner lid, check for any objects on the glass, verify the scanner lock is disengaged, and gently try to move the carriage by hand to check for obstructions.

causes.cause: Foreign object on scanner glass blocking carriage
Scanner carriage drive belt worn or broken

fixSteps.title: Check Scanner Glass
fixSteps.instruction: Open the scanner lid and remove any objects, paper clips, or debris from the scanner glass surface.

fixSteps.title: Check Scanner Lock
fixSteps.instruction: Look for a scanner transport lock (often a slider on the side or bottom of the scanner). Ensure it is in the unlocked position.

fixSteps.title: Clean Scanner Glass
fixSteps.instruction: Clean the scanner glass and the narrow strip of glass for the ADF with glass cleaner and a lint-free cloth.

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Close the scanner lid, turn off the printer, wait 30 seconds, and turn it back on. The scanner carriage will attempt to re-home during startup.

whenToCallPro: If the scanner carriage still cannot move after clearing obstructions, the drive belt or motor may need professional replacement.

faq.q: What causes the scanner carriage to get stuck?
faq.a: Usually a foreign object on the glass, the shipping lock being engaged, or debris from the document feeder.

faq.q: Can I still print if the scanner is stuck?
faq.a: Yes, printing functions are independent of the scanner. Only copy and scan features are affected.

faq.q: Do I need a service call?
faq.a: Check for obstructions and the transport lock first. If the carriage mechanism is physically broken, a technician is needed.

title: Transfer Belt Cleaner Worn
shortDescription: The transfer belt cleaning blade has worn out and needs replacement.
fullDescription:
Error 093-310 can also indicate the transfer belt cleaner blade assembly has reached its end of life. The cleaner blade removes residual toner from the transfer belt surface after each print cycle to prevent ghost images and contamination on subsequent prints.

When the cleaner blade wears out, you may notice ghost images, toner smears, or a gradual decrease in print quality. The blade is typically replaced as part of the transfer belt maintenance kit.

On most Xerox models, the cleaner blade is integrated into the transfer belt unit and is replaced along with the belt. Check if your model has a separate cleaner component.

causes.cause: Cleaner blade worn from normal use
Blade edge damaged by foreign material

symptoms: Toner smears on back of pages
Repeating marks at belt circumference interval

fixSteps.title: Verify Error Source
fixSteps.instruction: Check the supplies status on the control panel to confirm whether the transfer belt cleaner or the entire belt unit needs replacement.

fixSteps.title: Order Replacement Part
fixSteps.instruction: Order the transfer belt maintenance kit or cleaner unit for your specific printer model from Xerox or an authorized dealer.

fixSteps.title: Remove Old Unit
fixSteps.instruction: Open the front cover, remove the toner cartridges to access the transfer belt area, release the retention levers, and carefully remove the old belt/cleaner assembly.

fixSteps.title: Install New Unit
fixSteps.instruction: Install the new transfer belt/cleaner assembly, secure the retention levers, reinstall toner cartridges, and close the cover. The counter resets automatically.

whenToCallPro: Transfer belt replacement is user-serviceable, but call a pro if print quality issues persist after replacement.

faq.q: What is the transfer belt cleaner?
faq.a: It is a blade that removes residual toner from the transfer belt surface to prevent contamination on future prints.

faq.q: Is it replaced separately from the transfer belt?
faq.a: On most Xerox models, the cleaner is integrated into the belt unit and replaced together. Check your model's specifications.

faq.q: Do I need a service call?
faq.a: The transfer belt/cleaner kit is user-replaceable on most models. Professional service is only needed if quality issues persist.

title: SMB Scan Destination Error
shortDescription: The printer cannot connect to the configured SMB network folder for scan-to-folder.
fullDescription:
Error 024-950 indicates the printer failed to connect to a shared network folder when attempting to save a scanned document using the SMB (Server Message Block) protocol. The scan-to-folder function requires a valid network path, correct credentials, and proper share permissions.

This error commonly occurs when the target computer is turned off, the shared folder has been renamed or deleted, the user credentials have expired, or SMBv1 has been disabled on the server while the printer only supports SMBv1.

Verify the SMB share path, test the credentials from another device, and ensure the target computer allows SMB connections from the printer's IP address.

causes.cause: Incorrect SMB share path or credentials

symptoms: Scanned documents not saved to network

fixSteps.title: Verify Target Computer
fixSteps.instruction: Confirm the target computer or NAS is powered on, connected to the network, and the shared folder still exists.

fixSteps.title: Check SMB Configuration
fixSteps.instruction: Access the printer's EWS, navigate to the scan-to-folder settings, and verify the server address, share name, path, username, and password.

fixSteps.title: Test SMB Access
fixSteps.instruction: From another computer on the network, try accessing the shared folder using the same credentials to verify they work.

fixSteps.title: Check SMB Version
fixSteps.instruction: If the target runs Windows 10/11 or Server 2019+, ensure SMBv2/v3 is enabled. Some older Xerox printers may need SMBv1 enabled or a firmware update for SMBv2 support.

whenToCallPro: If you do not have access to the server or printer admin settings, contact your IT department.

faq.q: What does Xerox error 024-950 mean?
faq.a: The printer cannot connect to the network shared folder configured for scan-to-folder.

faq.q: Why did scan-to-folder stop working after a Windows update?
faq.a: Microsoft updates sometimes disable SMBv1. Check if your printer needs a firmware update for SMBv2 support.

faq.q: Do I need a service call?
faq.a: No, this is a network configuration issue. Contact your IT team if you cannot resolve the SMB connection settings.

title: Paper Size Mismatch
shortDescription: The paper loaded in the tray does not match the size configured in the print job.
fullDescription:
Error 005-275 indicates a mismatch between the paper size specified in the print job and the paper actually loaded in the selected tray. The printer detects paper size through the tray guides and sensors, and will pause the job when a discrepancy is found.

This commonly occurs when paper guides in the tray are not adjusted to match the loaded paper, when the print job specifies a size like Legal but the tray contains Letter, or when the tray paper size setting in the printer's configuration does not match the physical paper.

Adjust the paper guides in the tray to match the loaded paper, or change the paper size setting in the print driver to match what is actually in the tray.

causes.cause: Paper guides not adjusted to match paper size
Print job specifies different paper size than loaded
Tray paper size setting incorrect in printer config

symptoms: Paper size mismatch error on display
Print job paused waiting for correct paper
Prompt to load different paper size
Job prints on wrong size paper if overridden

fixSteps.title: Check Paper and Guides
fixSteps.instruction: Open the paper tray, verify the paper size loaded, and adjust the length and width guides to fit snugly against the paper stack.

fixSteps.title: Confirm Tray Settings
fixSteps.instruction: On the control panel, navigate to Device > Paper Trays and verify the paper size and type settings match the physical paper in each tray.

fixSteps.title: Check Print Driver Settings
fixSteps.instruction: On your computer, open the print dialog and verify the paper size setting matches the paper loaded in the target tray.

fixSteps.title: Override or Cancel
fixSteps.instruction: At the printer panel, you can override the mismatch and print on the loaded paper, or cancel the job and resubmit with correct settings.

affectedSeries: All Xerox models with paper trays
whenToCallPro: Only if the paper size sensor is malfunctioning and reports incorrect sizes even when guides are properly set.

faq.q: What does Xerox error 005-275 mean?
faq.a: The paper in the tray does not match the paper size your print job is expecting.

faq.q: Can I just press a button to continue printing?
faq.a: Yes, most Xerox models allow you to override the mismatch and print on whatever paper is loaded, but the output may be clipped or misaligned.

faq.q: Do I need a service call?
faq.a: No, this is a user configuration issue. Adjust the paper guides or print settings.

title: Laser Scanner Unit Error
shortDescription: The laser scanner (LSU) unit has malfunctioned.
fullDescription:
Error 062-790 indicates a failure in the laser scanner unit (LSU), which is the component that uses a laser beam and rotating polygon mirror to write the image onto the photoconductor drum. When the LSU fails, the printer cannot create any printed output.

The laser scanner unit is a precision optical component that spins at very high speeds. Failures can occur due to motor bearing wear, a dirty or damaged polygon mirror, or a laser diode failure. Dust accumulation inside the LSU housing can also cause intermittent failures.

This is typically a component that requires professional replacement, as it involves precision alignment and is located deep inside the printer. Power cycling may temporarily resolve intermittent LSU errors caused by motor startup failures.

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the printer, unplug it for 60 seconds, plug it back in, and turn it on. Some LSU errors are caused by motor startup failures that clear on retry.

fixSteps.title: Listen During Startup
fixSteps.instruction: During startup, listen for abnormal sounds such as grinding, clicking, or high-pitched whining from inside the printer. These indicate mechanical LSU failure.

fixSteps.title: Check for Overheating
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the printer has adequate ventilation. An overheating environment can cause the LSU motor to fail intermittently.

fixSteps.title: Contact Service
fixSteps.instruction: If the error persists after power cycling, the LSU unit needs professional replacement. Contact Xerox support or an authorized service provider.

whenToCallPro: Immediately if the error persists after a power cycle. The LSU is a precision component that requires professional replacement.

faq.q: What does Xerox error 062-790 mean?
faq.a: The laser scanner unit that writes images onto the drum has failed and likely needs professional replacement.

faq.q: Can I replace the laser scanner unit myself?
faq.a: This is not recommended. The LSU requires precise alignment and is deeply embedded inside the printer. Professional service is needed.

faq.q: Is it worth repairing or should I replace the printer?
faq.a: LSU replacement costs $200-500. Compare this to the cost of a new printer of the same model to decide.
